channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Reply to: From traditional to artificial intelligence-driven approaches: Revolutionizing personalized and precision nutrition in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Clinical nutrition ESPEN - Letter -

item.title: Comment on "From traditional to artificial intelligence-driven approaches: Revolutionizing personalized and precision nutrition in inflammatory bowel disease".
item.description: Clinical nutrition ESPEN - Letter -

item.title: Global Roll-Out and Continued Multi-Site Validation of the Artificial Intelligence Histology Instrument for Qualitative Assessment of Histopathology in Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: United European gastroenterology journal - Multicenter study - Histological assessment of mucosal biopsies in patients with ulcerative colitis (UC) can determine the activity and extent of disease and assess response to treatment. However, its widespread adopt...

item.title: Pharmacokinetic characteristics of Bletilla Striata active components in rats and 1H NMR metabolomics study on ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Fitoterapia - This study aimed to systematically investigate the pharmacokinetic characteristics of Bletilla striata extract (BSE) in rats, and its metabolomic regulatory mechanism in ulcerative colitis (UC) mod...

item.title: A Novel Endoscopic Biopsy Technique for Translational Research in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Clinical and translational gastroenterology - Standard forceps biopsies often provide insufficient cellular yield for single-cell and spatial transcriptomic analyses. We assessed the feasibility of cold-snare resection in inflammatory bowel di...

item.title: Peripheral κ opioid receptor in pain and inflammation: From molecular signalling and gene expression to drug discovery.
item.description: British journal of pharmacology - Review - The κ opioid receptor (κ receptor, KOR) is a G protein-coupled receptor with well established roles in analgesia and immune modulation. Although historically studied primarily in the central nervou...

item.title: Microbial metabolite Urolithin A protects against inorganic arsenic-induced gut barrier dysfunction in humanized AS3MT mice.
item.description: Gut microbes - Chronic exposure to inorganic arsenic (iAs) remains a major environmental health concern and is associated with significant gastrointestinal (GI) disorders, including gastroenteritis, diarrhea, and...

item.title: Dual Targeted Therapy for Refractory Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Journal of evidence-based medicine - Emerging evidence suggests that dual targeted therapy (DTT), which employs two biologics or small molecules concurrently, may improve outcomes for patients with refractory inflammatory bowel diseas...

item.title: Evolving Research Hotspots and Emerging Trends in Atopic Dermatitis and Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Bibliometric Analysis.
item.description: Clinical, cosmetic and investigational dermatology - The number of people affected by atopic dermatitis (AD) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) continues to rise, increasing the disease burden. These conditions are epidemiologically linked and shar...

item.title: TIME-trend and forecasting of the economic impact of inflammatory bowel disease in Catalonia: a population-based analysis.
item.description: Journal of medical economics - Few studies have assessed the distribution and temporal trends of healthcare expenditures related to inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). The aims of the study were to analyze the expenditure trends f...

item.title: Folate and global health review series, part 4: syntheses on folate and autoimmune diseases and skeletal outcomes.
item.description: Journal of global health - Review - Autoimmune diseases and bone density loss (osteopenia and osteoporosis) are chronic conditions of complex aetiology that affect diverse populations. Folate may be associated with a higher risk of t...

item.title: Influenza and herpes zoster vaccine effectiveness in autoimmune or immune-mediated diseases.
item.description: Journal of infection and public health - Individuals with autoimmune or immune mediated diseases (AID) are more susceptible to infections and often experience worse outcomes if infected, underscoring the importance of vaccination. However...

item.title: Risk of Myeloproliferative Neoplasms in Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Impact on Outcomes: A Multi-Centre Matched Analysis.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPNs) share chronic inflammation and immune dysregulation. We evaluated the incidence of MPNs among patients with IBD and clinical...

item.title: International collaborative experiences in managing pyoderma gangrenosum in patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - Case report - Pyoderma gangrenosum (PG) is a rare but challenging extraintestinal manifestation (EIM) of inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD), affecting 6%-48% of IBD patients. This retrospective study analyzed aff...

item.title: The burden and unique patterns of extraintestinal manifestations in Chinese inflammatory bowel disease: a nationwide multicenter investigation.
item.description: Clinical and experimental medicine - Extraintestinal manifestations (EIMs) are a significant disease burden in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, large-scale epidemiological data from the Chinese population remain scarce. This...

item.title: Lactylation-Related Genes in Ulcerative Colitis: A Multiomics Mendelian Randomization Study for Therapeutic Target Discovery.
item.description: Human mutation - Ulcerative colitis (UC), a chronic and nonspecific intestinal inflammatory disease, has seen a rising incidence rate worldwide year by year. Its pathogenesis remains incompletely understood. Lactyl...

item.title: Exploring the genetic correlation between inflammatory bowel disease and psychiatric disorders: insights from genome-wide association studies.
item.description: European archives of psychiatry and clinical neuroscience - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and psychiatric disorders exhibit complex comorbid patterns, with anxiety and depressive disorders being significantly more prevalent in IBD patients compared to th...

item.title: Performance of Google NotebookLM for AI-assisted data extraction and consensus statement generation in a heterogenous systematic review on inflammatory bowel disease, obesity, and cardiometabolic comorbidities: A Methodological Report.
item.description: medRxiv : the preprint server for health sciences - Large language models (LLMs) offer promise for systematic review data extraction, but performance in complex multidisciplinary domains and utility for clinical statement generation remain insuffici...

item.title: Multifocal autoimmune pancreatitis complicated by pancreatic insufficiency and segmental portal hypertension: A case report and review of the literature.
item.description: Radiology case reports - Case report - Autoimmune pancreatitis is an increasingly recognized variant of chronic pancreatitis, classified into 2 subtypes by the international consensus diagnostic criteria: type 1, associated with IgG4 (I...

item.title: Pharmacological approaches for colitis associated colorectal cancer: a survey in preclinical studies.
item.description: Cancer treatment and research communications - Review - Colitis-associated colorectal cancer (CAC) is a severe complication of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), characterized by a unique inflammation-driven pathogenesis that distinguishes it from sporad...
